data_in.description: msimat object: preprocessed MSI data matrix with intensity values for each mass peak across all pixels
massdiff object annotated with adductMatch() and filtered by corrPairsMSI() for spatial correlation significance
correlation-tested massdiff object with Estimate, P.value, and Significance columns from corrPairsMSI()

data_out.description: Annotated mass spectrum plot with parent ions marked as blue circle outlines and adduct ions marked as red filled points, showing overlap indicating ions with dual roles

domain_knowledge: In MALDI-MSI, sodium adducts form when sodium ions attach to neutral molecules during ionization, producing peaks at mass m/z = [M+Na]+ with the characteristic mass difference of 22.99 Da from the parent molecular ion.

Spatial correlation testing in MSI exploits pixel-level intensity covariance between parent and putative adduct peaks; high Pearson correlation coefficients (after Bonferroni multiple-testing correction) indicate that the two peaks co-occur spatially, supporting a true adduct relationship rather than chance mass-difference matching.

The overlap of red and blue annotation points on a mass spectrum indicates molecular ions that serve a dual role: they are simultaneously the product adduct of a lighter parent ion AND the parent ion for a heavier adduct, creating a chain of related molecular species.

The pointsAdducts() function requires prior annotation of peak pairs with adductMatch() and significance filtering via corrPairsMSI(); calling it without this preprocessing will result in empty or misleading visualizations.

Visual inspection of the annotated spectrum is a key validation step: the expected red/blue overlap pattern confirms that the workflow correctly identified ions with multiple adduct relationships in the same biological sample.

script_runs: execute pointsAdducts(d, d.diff.annot.cor.Na, which="adduct", signif=TRUE, pch=20, cex=0.5, col="red") on a correlation-filtered massdiff object subset to Na adduct matches without error

file_format_is: output figure in standard graphics format (PNG, PDF, or R graphics object)
expert_review: visual assessment that red/blue overlap pattern in scatter plot is consistent with reported paper findings regarding adduct-parent ion co-localization
confirmation that pointsAdducts() correctly interprets the which="adduct" parameter to distinguish adduct from parent ion populations
assessment that signif=TRUE filtering parameter appropriately restricts display to statistically significant correlation pairs

[other] massdiff object annotated with adductMatch() and filtered by corrPairsMSI() for spatial correlation significance: 'd.diff.annot <- adductMatch(d.diff,add=adducts2)'

[other] Annotated mass spectrum plot with parent ions marked as blue circle outlines and adduct ions marked as red filled points, showing overlap indicating ions with dual roles: 'You can annotate the original mass spectrum using a massdiff object, to mark peaks corresponding to parent ions and adduct ions in different colors.'
[discussion] No changelog documenting version history, breaking changes, or function signature stability for pointsAdducts() across releases: '_No changelog found._'

cmd: library(mass2adduct); msimat <- readRDS('{inputs.msimat_data}'); massdiff_cor <- readRDS('{inputs.massdiff_annotated_cor}'); massdiff_na <- massdiff_cor[massdiff_cor$adduct == '[M+Na]+', ]; png('adduct_parent_overlap_plot.png', width=800, height=600); plot(msimat@mass, msimat@intensity[1,], type='n', xlab='m/z', ylab='Intensity', main='Sodium Adduct-Parent Ion Spatial Overlap'); pointsAdducts(massdiff_na, which='parent', pch=1, col='blue', cex=1.5); pointsAdducts(massdiff_na, which='adduct', pch=20, col='red', cex=1.2); legend('topright', c('Parent ions', 'Adduct ions'), pch=c(1, 20), col=c('blue', 'red')); dev.off(); cat('Plot saved to adduct_parent_overlap_plot.png\n')

task_description: Generate a scatter plot annotation of the mass spectrum highlighting sodium adduct ions (red points) and their parent ions (blue outlines) from a correlation-filtered, adduct-matched massdiff object, showing the red/blue overlap pattern where some adduct ions serve as parents for further adducts.
task_objective: Generate a scatter plot using pointsAdducts() on correlation-filtered Na adduct matches to visualize parent and adduct ion distributions with color coding.

task_outputs.label: Mass spectrum with sodium adduct parent/adduct ion annotation

title: Analyze adduct-parent ion spatial overlap using pointsAdducts() on Na adduct annotations

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Annotated mass spectrum plot with parent ions marked as blue circle outlines and adduct ions marked as red filled points, showing overlap indicating ions with dual roles.
workflow_description: 1. Load the preprocessed msimat object (MSI data matrix) and the correlation-tested massdiff object annotated with adductMatch() results. 2. Subset the massdiff object to sodium adduct matches only using subset(d.diff.annot.cor, matches=='Na adduct'). 3. Invoke pointsAdducts() with which='adduct', signif=TRUE, pch=20, cex=0.5, col='red' to overlay adduct ion peaks in red. 4. Invoke pointsAdducts() again with which='parent', signif=TRUE, pch=1, cex=0.5, col='blue' to overlay parent ion peaks as blue circle outlines. 5. Display the annotated mass spectrum plot showing overlapping red and blue points indicating ions serving dual roles (adduct and parent).
